reasoning: The historical trend for the national average interest rate for savings accounts (SNDR) over the past year has been generally flat to slightly downward, starting at 0.43% in November 2024 and reaching 0.40% by late 2025. Over the last three recorded months (Sep, Oct, Nov), the rate has continuously stayed at exactly 0.40%. Given that the broader US monetary policy environment points toward a gradual easing (rate cuts), there is substantial downward pressure on deposit rates. Savings rates are unlikely to increase aggressively in this context. However, short-term deposit rates can exhibit minor localized fluctuations (as seen earlier in August-September 2025 when they bounced from 0.38% to 0.40%). Following the instructions that uncertainty should increase with the forecast horizon, I am setting the first resolution date (which is just one month away) at a low probability of 22%, assuming the rate will likely remain flat at 0.40% or decrease gently as banks adjust to recent rate cuts. For the second resolution date in February 2026, the 3-month window provides more room for variance and random walk drift (even if the overall trend is down), so I bring the probability closer to 0.5, to 36%.
